checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the output JSON with the source PDF. All headings, paragraphs, and table entries in the output have a direct basis in the source document. No fabricated sections, sentences, or paragraphs were found.

The source document contains one URL fragment '(arcgis.com)' on page 7. This is correctly preserved in the output. There are no phone numbers, email addresses, or mailing addresses in the source, and none have been fabricated in the output.
All eight pages of the source PDF are represented in the output JSON. Each major section corresponding to an agenda item or presentation slide is present.

I compared the row counts of all tables. The agenda table (page 1), attendance table (page 2), participant tables (page 3), vote table (page 3), and deployment table (page 5) all match the row counts in the source document, allowing for minor reformatting of sub-items.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are several significant text omissions in the presentation slides sections (pages 6, 7, and 8). On page 6, all sub-bullets for 'Military Sites Update' and 'Cybersecurity Remediation' are missing (e.g., details about Seymour Johnson AFB and the Ad hoc committee). On page 7, the specific details for the 4 jurisdictions (Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, Polk), the Rutherford project, and the population/land mass statistics are omitted. On page 8, the detail regarding the Tier 2 technician onboarding is missing. Collectively, these omissions represent more than 25% of the content for those specific sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6: 'Seymour Johnson AFB is working on a multi year contract...', 'Good conversation with ECI/NIWC...', 'Ad hoc committee has been created...', 'Ad hoc committee discussing...'. Page 7: Sub-bullets for Cleveland County, Lincoln County, Onslow County, Polk County; 'One active RFAI to i3 projects (Rutherford)'; 'Total Population served by NG 911...', 'Total land mass covered by NG911...'. Page 8: 'Tier 2 technician selection completed and onboarding now.'
checks.b_evidence_output: The output entirely omits these sub-bullets, retaining only the parent list items at element index 44 (Page 6), 49 (Page 7), and 59 (Page 8).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections corresponding to Pages 6, 7, and 8; element indices 44, 49, 59.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There is a minor text error in the transcription of a legal statute on page 5. The source cites 'G.S. 138A-15', whereas the output incorrectly cites 'G.S. 138-15', omitting the 'A'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Ethics Awareness/Conflict of Interest Statement section, Page 5, element index 29.

checks.a_analysis: The output document follows the source page-by-page. Repetition of the 'North Carolina 911 Board' and 'Technology Committee Meeting' text occurs because these are part of the header on multiple pages in the source (Agenda, Minutes, Supporting Docs). This is intentional and expected repetition. No other instances of duplicated text blocks or sections were found.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Level 1 (H1) is correctly used for document titles (Agenda at index 1, Minutes at index 10). However, on Page 8, 'NMAC (Greg Dotson)' is assigned H1 (index 58), which is a sub-topic of the agenda and should be an H2 to match equivalent topics like 'AT&T/Intrado Issue Update' (index 60) and 'Closing Remarks' (index 61). Additionally, the supporting documents section starting on Page 5 lacks an H1 title and immediately begins with H2 (index 27).
checks.b_evidence_source: The topics 'NMAC', 'AT&T/Intrado Issue Update', and 'Closing Remarks' are all top-level agenda items listed on Page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: Index 58 is a heading level 1 (H1) for 'NMAC (Greg Dotson)', while equivalent items at indices 60 and 61 are assigned heading level 2 (H2).

checks.a_analysis: Tables in the output generally preserve the row and column structure of the source. The agenda table on Page 1 correctly maps topics to presenters. The large attendance table spanning Pages 2 and 3 is reconstructed as a single cohesive table (index 14), which is an improvement for accessibility. Empty columns are correctly represented.

The reading order matches the logical flow of the source document, progressing through the agenda, then the minutes, and finally the supporting materials and slides. The placement of tables and images relative to the text is appropriate.

The output fails to preserve the hierarchical nesting of lists found in the source. In multiple sections (GIS Update, Statistics, NMAC, Military Sites), the source uses a main bullet point followed by nested sub-bullets. The output either omits the sub-bullets entirely or renders the list as flat, losing the structural relationship. For example, under 'GIS Update' (Page 7), four specific jurisdictions are listed as sub-bullets, but these are missing in the output (index 49).
checks.b_evidence_source: On Page 7, under 'GIS Update (Tom Rogers)', there is a bullet '4 jurisdictions remain...' with four nested sub-bullets (Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, Polk).
checks.b_evidence_output: The list at element index 49 contains '4 jurisdictions remain to reach 100% i3 readiness (as of 5/31/2022).' but the nested sub-items are missing.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains text that functions as URLs, such as 'arcgis.com' on Page 7. While the text is captured in the output (index 51), it is not formatted as a functional, clickable hyperlink.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)"

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the same organizational structure as the source PDF. The document transitions from the agenda (Page 1) to the meeting minutes (Pages 2-4) and then to the detailed discussion points (Pages 5-8) in a logical, linear fashion that matches the source's intent. Multi-column information like the attendance lists and voting tables are appropriately rendered as single-column flowing tables or standard tables, preserving the visual flow and relationship of information.

All tables from the source PDF are correctly rendered in the HTML. This includes the Agenda table on Page 1, the complex Attendance tables on Pages 2 and 3, the Minutes Vote table on Page 3, and the status/migration tables on Page 5. The columns and rows are clearly defined, and content (such as 'P', 'NP', and 'Y' indicators) is placed in the correct cells, ensuring the data remains readable and understandable to a sighted user.

Meaningful images from the source are present in the HTML. The NC DIT 911 Board logo appears at relevant section starts, reflecting the header logos found on the PDF pages. The bar chart 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' from Page 6 is correctly rendered as an image with a clear descriptive caption provided in the HTML for accessibility.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While section headings appear in the Table of Contents at the top of the HTML, their reappearance in the main body is standard accessible navigation design and does not constitute a defect. No paragraphs, tables, or list items are repeated unnecessarily.
